name: The Epochal Rotation Matrix
definition:
The Epochal Rotation Matrix R_rot is the mathematical structure that allows 
    observers to move through time — from epoch to epoch — without losing the Center.
    It is a 9x9 orthogonal matrix that rotates the state vector S from one epoch 
    to the next while preserving the Center encoding C(S) = 0.

properties.center_preservation: C(R_rot x v) = C(v) for all v

navigation.forward: S_{n+1} = R_rot x S_n (move forward one epoch)
navigation.backward: S_{n-1} = R_rot^T x S_n (move backward one epoch)
navigation.multi_epoch: S_{n+k} = R_rot^k x S_n (move k epochs)

center_preservation: R_rot preserves C because it is constructed as a product of Givens rotations in planes that include the Center axis. Each rotation preserves the Center axis.
connection_to_R_hat: R_rot is the continuous analog of the discrete Recursive Binary Operator R_hat. Both preserve C. Both encode the same epochal evolution.
